hypothesis_en: Mr President, the Commission today sees that the question of gender balance exists, although not so much as a whole, and what it needs to know is that this is maintained and increased, and I hope that the next Commission, although, in the end, what we have seen of the ten new proposals, there is a certain imbalance, but what are we going to do? What you are proposing, Your Honour, is that Afghanistan does not appear specifically contemplated, which does not mean that we are not going to continue our action in Afghanistan. Iraq is not exactly the same as Afghanistan, Afghanistan is much poorer, it has much less potential and natural resources of its own, which, when Iraq recovers its infrastructure and can put its potential into action, undoubtedly opens up a much more promising horizon, and what we all hope is that it can be in that situation as soon as possible. Of course, we must not start inventing the wheel every two days, and I fully agree with you that what is needed is to comply with what was agreed in Lisbon, to implement and develop in all the countries of the Union the reforms that were put forward there, and that this is how we will increase the competitiveness of our economy, and that next year we will have to make the report halfway through. It will have to be seen, among other things, whether the action at Community level has been in line with the objectives. I believe, of course, that in some areas that I know well, such as transport and energy, everything is practically culminated with something that we have in the last procedures between Parliament and the Council, and not only in this area, but in other areas. There are still some points to be done, but above all where we are failing is in the implementation by the Member States of these structural measures, where there is a very unbalanced progress. There are countries that have advanced very quickly and there are others that still have some difficulties, but we hope that in the coming times we are all aware of the need to add. I hope that these reforms will be accelerated and carried out as soon as possible, because the European Union needs them.
reference_en: (1.05) thank you Mr President well (4.74) the question of gender balance is cer= certainly respected in the Commission at least the Commission's attendance today I hope the next Commission will be even more balanced but anyway of the ten new proposals of the ten new proposals Commission has to pass a degree of imbalance but anyway that's by the by we need to talk about realities not quotas anyway to the heart of the matter raised by you Mr Deva (1.26) let me say that the fact that Afghanistan is not specifically mentioned does not mean that we will not continue targeting Afghanistan for action we have commitments to Afghanistan and we will continue to deliver on them the problem is that we have to stump up for the reconstruction of Iraq (1.66) the situation of the country Iraq is very different to that of Afghanistan Afghanistan is far poorer there it does not have much in the way of natural resources which is of course not the case in Iraq particularly when the infrastructures are back up and running so that they can use these resources and that of course m= gives grounds for hope in that country and we hope that that situation will attain as soon as possible now as far as the l= the the Lisbon agenda ladies and gentlemen quite so we shouldn't reinvent the wheel every other day I wholeheartedly agree with you what we need to do is (1.11) do what we've agreed to do in Lisbon and and to carry out the reforms agreed to there in all countries of the European Union and that's the way we will manage to increase the competitiveness of the European economy and next year when we have to draw up the the mid-term review (1.6) one of the issues we will have to consider is whether Community action has actually helped us meet these objectives in some areas such as transport and energy which are areas with which I'm familiar I think we've done well we've pretty much done everything we were supposed to do with with a few minor details yet (1.09) and there're issues these issues under discussion between Council and Parliament but there's still more to be done of course but where we are failing mainly is in getting Member States to actually take (2.3) these structural measures there's a great imbalance some countries have moved down very quickly other countries are still having some problems we're all aware of the need to move forward w= I've heard statements by heads of state and government recently along these lines I hope that these reforms will be stepped up and accelerated because they they are reforms that the EU urgently needs thank you Mr President
source_es: gracias señor Presidente bueno la Comisión hoy se ve que la cuestión del equilibrio del gender balance existe aunque en el conjunto pues no tanto y lo que tiene que saber es que esto se mantenga y se incremente y espero que la próxima Comisión aunque en fin lo que hemos visto de los diez nuevas propuestas pues quizá allá hay un cierto desequilibrio pero bueno qué le vamos a hacer no se trata de hablar de cuotas sino de realidades yendo a lo que plantea su Señoría (1.01) decir que el que Afganistán no aparezca específicamente contemplado no quiere decir que no no vamos a mantener su= la acción en Afganistán aquí tenemos un progra= unos compromisos que se van a mantener lógicamente lo que evidentemente lo que pla= pasa es que planteamos la contribución de la Unión al proceso de reconstrucción de Iraq aunque tiene razón que la s= situación las posibilidades de un país como Iraq no es exactamente los mismos que Afganistán Afganistán es muchísimo más pobre tiene muchas menos posibilidades y recursos naturales propios cosa que Iraq cuando se recupere sus infraestructuras y pueda poner en marcha sus potencialidades indudablemente abre un horizonte mucho más esperanzador y lo que esperamos todos es que cuanto antes se pueda estar en ~esa_ situación en cuanto a la Agenda de Lisboa Señorías efectivamente no hay que empezar a inventar la rueda cada dos días yo coincido plenamente con su Señoría que lo que hace falta es cumplir con lo que se acordó en Lisboa cumplir llevar a cabo y desarrollar en todos los países de la Unión las reformas que allí se plantearon y que a= por ese camino es cómo vamos a conseguir incrementar la competitividad de nuestra economía y que desde luego el año que viene que habrá que hacer el informe a medio ~recorrirido de medio recorrido tendrá que verse entre otras cosas si (1.07) la actuación a nivel comunitario ha estado a la altura de los objetivos yo creo desde luego en algunos áreas que conozco bien como por ejemplo el transporte y la energía está prácticamente todo culminado con alguna cosa que tenemos en co= en los últimos trámites entre del Parlamento y el el Consejo y no sólo en ese terreno sino en otros terrenos quedan algunos puntos por hacer pero sobre todo donde estamos fallando es en la puesta en la realización por parte de los Estados de la Unión de esas medidas estructurales donde ahí hay un avance muy desequilibrado hay países que han avanzado muy rápido y hay otros que todavía tienen algunas dificultades pero esperamos que en los próximos tiempos somos todos conscientes de la necesidad de hacer no se ha oído algunas declaraciones en los últimos tiempos de primeros ministros en ese sentido y jefes de gobierno y presidentes espero que esas reformas se aceleren y se lleven a cabo cuanto antes porque la Unión Europea las necesita muchas gracias señor Presidente
